data.description:
## Context
The reconciler keeps two kinds of shared, mutable state at the root of the shared `tickets` branch: `.bridge_state/bindings.json` (the Jira-key <-> rebar-id mapping DATA) and `.reconciler-pass-lock`/`.reconciler-phase-gate` (cross-clone COORDINATION). Because both are mutable non-disjoint paths rewritten each pass, two clones produce divergent edits -> union-merge conflict, today papered over with a `merge=ours` git driver that SILENTLY DISCARDS one clone's write (a data-loss band-aid). Supersedes orphan 0241 (long-lath-fairy); discovered from the closed epic 97e7 (whose WU-3 shipped `merge=ours` as the deliberate-but-stopgap mitigation).

This session researched how mature projects converge such state (Dolt, git-bug, Fossil, Automerge/Yjs, Riak/Dynamo, etcd/Consul/ZooKeeper/Kubernetes/DynamoDB-lock-client) and ran local git experiments. Findings: append-only uuid records converge structurally with zero loss; `merge=ours` was confirmed to silently drop a real write; git ref CAS gives atomic cross-clone mutual exclusion; a relative-duration lease + heartbeat + CAS-break-on-stale is the converged self-healing-lock pattern (etcd/Consul/ZK/K8s/Redisson/DynamoDB all use lease+renewal at ~TTL/3, steal-on-expiry via CAS).

The design splits along a data-vs-coordination seam and honors the local-vs-cross-environment principle: both artifacts are cross-environment, so both must be concurrency-robust and self-healing.

## Success Criteria
- Union merge of two unrelated/diverged reconciler histories is conflict-free on ALL paths without `merge=ours`, and an orphaned reconciler lock self-heals without human intervention — with reconcile idempotency preserved as the load-bearing safety invariant.

## Workstreams
- WS-1 (convergent bindings, DATA): model bindings as append-only `BIND`/`UNBIND`(tombstone)/`REMAP` events (reuse the store's existing union-merge + HLC). Current mapping = a read-time fold with **HLC-latest-wins** for the rare same-key tie; **tombstones** for retirement (never row-delete); optional writer-provenance tags to suppress the two-pipeline echo loop. Then DELETE the `merge=ours` `.gitattributes` (provably unnecessary once convergent). SCOPED OUT (future, optional): proactive conflict-surfacing of a genuinely-concurrent same-key remap — Layer 1 already makes it non-destructive (loser event stays in history), so a read-only `bridge_fsck` warning can be added later if same-key collisions are ever observed.
- WS-2 (self-healing ref-lock, COORDINATION): move `.reconciler-pass-lock`/`-phase-gate` off the tickets tree to a dedicated bare ref `refs/reconciler/lock` -> blob (NOT git-notes, which opts into union machinery). Create-only CAS acquire (`update-ref <ref> <blob> 0{40}` / explicit `--force-with-lease=ref:` over the wire — never the bare lease form). Lock blob carries holder + **relative** lease duration (clock-skew-proof, DynamoDB-lock-client pattern) + HLC heartbeat + monotonic fence counter (diagnostic). Heartbeat renews at ~lease/3; a contender CAS-breaks only a lease-expired lock (race-free: exactly one breaker wins). Authoritative ref lives on `origin`. Framed honestly as an EFFICIENCY lock (safe only because reconcile is idempotent), not a correctness lock.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bindings are append-only `BIND`/`UNBIND`(tombstone)/`REMAP` events; current state is an HLC-latest-wins read fold; tombstones handle retirement; no mutable `bindings.json` blob remains
- [ ] The `merge=ours` `.gitattributes` for `.bridge_state/*` + `.reconciler-*` is removed; union merge of two diverged histories is conflict-free on all paths (regression test)
- [ ] Reconciler lock/gate live in `refs/reconciler/*` (bare ref -> blob, not in the working tree / not union-merged); create-only CAS acquire, CAS release
- [ ] Stale-lock self-heal: relative-duration lease + heartbeat@~lease/3 + CAS-break-on-expiry; a double-break race resolves to exactly one winner (test)
- [ ] Reconcile pass idempotency is preserved/asserted (the invariant that makes the efficiency lock safe); no new _engine_support -> reconciler coupling unless justified
- [ ] Full non-integration suite green
data.title: Conflict-free reconciler state: convergent bindings + self-healing ref-locks (retire merge=ours)
